clusters.title: Who is new Apple CEO John Ternus?
clusters.neutral_headline: Who is new Apple CEO John Ternus

items.title: Who is new Apple CEO John Ternus?
items.standfirst: After 15 years in charge Tim Cook has named well-liked Apple engineer John Ternus as his successor.
items.content: After 15 years in charge Tim Cook has named well-liked Apple engineer John Ternus as his successor.

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down after 15 years
items.standfirst: Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved with Apple.
items.content: Apple has announced its biggest leadership change in 15 years, with Tim Cook set to hand over the role of Chief Executive to hardware boss John Ternus on September 1, 2026. Cook, who took over from the late Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain involved in the company as Executive Chairman, focused on policy engagement and board-level responsibilities. Ternus, 50, has spent almost his entire career at Apple, joining in 2001 and most recently serving as Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering.LIVE UPDATES: Trump says he's 'highly unlikely' to renew ceasefireHe led the teams behind the iPad, AirPods, and the latest iPhone lineup, including the newly released iPhone Air. Under Cook's leadership, Apple grew its market capitalisation from roughly $350 billion to $4 trillion — a more than tenfold increase. The company now operates in more than 200 countries, and its products are used by more than 2.5 billion people worldwide. "It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company," Cook said in a statement. "I love Apple with all of my being, and I am so grateful to have had the opportunity to work with a team of such ingenious, innovative, creative, and deeply caring people who have been unwavering in their dedication to enriching the lives of our customers and creating the best products and services in the world.READ MORE: Inside 'kitty flipping', the recreational drug trend booming in Aussie cities"John Ternus has the mind of an engineer, the soul of an innovator, and the heart to lead with integrity and with honour. He is a visionary whose contributions to Apple over 25 years are already too numerous to count, and he is without question the right person to lead Apple into the future. "I could not be more confident in his abilities and his character, and I look forward to working closely with him on this transition and in my new role as executive chairman." The transition was approved unanimously by Apple's board of directors. Arthur Levinson, Apple's non-executive chairman for the past 15 years, will move to the role of Lead Independent Director. Ternus will join the board when he becomes CEO.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Queensland man killed in workplace accident involving forklift
items.standfirst: Emergency services were called to Karreman Quarries on West Mount Cotton Road in Sheldon about 2.20pm yesterday following reports a man had been critically injured in a forklift incident.
items.content: A worker has been killed in a horrific workplace accident involving a forklift in south-east Queensland.Emergency services were called to Karreman Quarries on West Mount Cotton Road in Sheldon, located around 30 kilometres from Brisbane, about 2.20pm yesterday following reports a man had been critically injured in a forklift incident.The man died from his injuries, a spokesperson for Resources, Safety and Health Queensland (RSHQ) confirmed today.EXCLUSIVE: 'Demoralising': Jacques has faced over 100 job rejections after redundancyREAD MORE: Bodyguard allegedly snatched from home and assaulted in botched abduction "Our thoughts are with the worker's family, friends and colleagues at this time," the spokesperson said."Enquiries into the incident are under way."As this is an active investigation no further comment can be made at this time."NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

clusters.title: Masked youths carry out broad daylight attack with machetes, sledgehammer
clusters.neutral_headline: Masked youths carry out broad daylight attack with machetes, sledgehammer

items.title: Masked youths carry out broad daylight attack with machetes, sledgehammer
items.standfirst: CCTV footage shows a group of balaclava-clad young people hanging out of the sunroof of a car as it drove down Bay Street in Port Melbourne yesterday afternoon.
items.content: Harrowing scenes unfolded on an inner-city street in Melbourne yesterday as a gang of masked youths allegedly used poles, a machete and a sledgehammer to attack two people in broad daylight.CCTV footage shows a group of balaclava-clad young people hanging out of the sunroof of a car as it drove down Bay Street in Port Melbourne before the attack yesterday afternoon.It's believed they were seaching for their targets. The car then stopped and several people, including one person appearing to carry a large knife, jumped out and began running.READ MORE: Jacques has applied for over 100 jobs. He says AI is making it impossible to stand outREAD MORE: Dezi Freeman's hideout: A look inside Australia's most infamous shipping containerPolice were then called to reports of an attack on Lalor Street, located just a few hundred metres away, about 3.15pm.It is understood the group of six hooded thugs chased two young people down the street. Witnesses said the group violently attacked the pair with a pole and a machete."It was a ruckus... there was screaming," resident Hilary told 9News."I left SA to escape the violence... I moved to the UK, I witnessed the same thing and now it's right outside my front door in Australia."A tradie who attempted to intervene by throwing a sledgehammer at the fray said one of the thugs took it and used it as a weapon. Families say they were cowering inside their homes in fear as the violent scenes took place on their doorstep. 3AW's Ross Stevenson said he has "never seen" an incident like this in Melbourne."It looks like a scene from Syria," he said of the footage. Witnesses said the group fled the area in a white Haval SUV, which was later spotted at Junction Oval in St Kilda.The group, including the targets of the attack, left the scene before police arrived.READ MORE: Inside 'kitty flipping', the recreational drug trend booming in Australian cities"No victims have come forward to report the incident to police," a spokesperson for Victoria Police said."There were no reports of weapons involved."The car has not been found, police added. No arrests have been made. NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

clusters.title: Bodyguard's alleged kidnappers arrested after dramatic chase
clusters.neutral_headline: Bodyguard's alleged kidnappers arrested after dramatic chase

items.title: Bodyguard's alleged kidnappers arrested after dramatic chase
items.standfirst: The victim – believed to be the bodyguard of a figure who is understood to have links to the Alameddine crime network in Sydney – was forced into his own car outside his home in Guildford.
items.content: Five masked men who allegedly abducted and assaulted a gangland associate in Sydney overnight were caught and arrested within one hour during a midnight raid.The victim, a bodyguard believed to be linked to a senior Alameddine crime network figure, was kidnapped from his home in Guildford and bundled into his own car about 11.30pm yesterday.The group of alleged offenders had no idea that police were tracking their every move from the sky.LIVE UPDATES: Trump may have let slip the real reason for his war READ MORE: 'Kitty flipping' sounds cute. It's a killer invading AustraliaThe victim was driven to Villawood, where the alleged offenders swapped cars.His dumped Ford Ranger was found partially burnt-out a short time later.Police then swooped on the group about 12.30am after following them via PolAir to a house in Casula in the city's south-west.Officers broke into the home and rescued the victim.He was treated for facial injuries and was taken to hospital in a stable condition.A group of five men were then arrested near the Hume Highway in Casula with the assistance of the Raptor Squad.Footage obtained by 9News shows police, including members of the Tactical Operations Unit, shows officers searching a nearby safehouse.MONEY: Millions of Australian workers to get instant $1000 tax deductionThe arrested men have been taken to Liverpool Station.No charges have been laid.A crime scene has been established and an investigation into the alleged abduction and assault is under way. Police are now investigating if the incident is linked to a brewing underworld battle in Sydney. NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

clusters.title: One dead and six injured after shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids
clusters.neutral_headline: One dead and six injured after shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids

items.title: One dead and six injured after shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids
items.standfirst: A Canadian tourist has died and at least six other people have been injured after a man opened fire while atop of one of the historic Teotihuacán pyramids in Mexico.
items.content: A Canadian tourist has died and at least six other people have been injured after a man opened fire while atop of one of the historic Teotihuacán pyramids in Mexico.

items.title: Tourist shot dead, others injured at historic pyramid
items.standfirst: A famous historical site in Mexico City, popular with tourists, has been rocked by tragedy.
items.content: A Canadian tourist has been killed and multiple others injured after a gunman opened fire at a historic site in Mexico.Local authorities said the man began shooting at the Teotihuacan pyramids before turning the gun on himself and dying from a self-inflicted gunshot wound.Mexican authorities say four people were wounded by gunshots, and two more were injured from falling on the pyramid during the ensuing chaos.LIVE UPDATES: Trump says he's 'highly unlikely' to renew ceasefireOfficers working security at the site were the first to respond, followed by a National Guard unit in a van. Among the injured are Colombian, Russian and Canadian tourists.A tour guide told the Associated Press dozens of tourists were at the top of the Pyramid of the Moon when a man standing on the structure's platform began firing upward. Video and photos published by local news organisations show a man standing with a gun on a platform while people above duck for cover.A number of gunshots ring out in the videos."Some people, because they were scared ... threw themselves face down on the ground, and the rest of us started to go down," the guide said, recounting how the shooter, upon seeing the tourists descending the pyramid's steps, began firing.READ MORE: Bodyguard allegedly snatched from home in botched Sydney abductionMexican President Claudia Sheinbaum wrote on social media that the shooting would be investigated and that she was in touch with the Canadian Embassy."What happened today in Teotihuacán deeply pains us. I express my most sincere solidarity with the affected individuals and their families," she wrote.Anita Anand, Canada's foreign affairs minister, said on X that as a "result of a horrific act of gun violence, a Canadian was killed and another wounded in Teotihuacán" and that her "thoughts are with their family and loved ones".The Teotihuacán pyramids are a series of massive structures on the outskirts of Mexico City built by three different ancient civilisations.As one of Mexico's most important tourist destinations, the site drew more than 1.8 million international visitors last year, according to government figures.Reported with Associated Press NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
